article_meta.title: Item 1 of 7 Texas State Troopers line up during a protest at the South Texas Family Residential Center, where Adrian Con

reasoning: The model repeatedly flagged quoted/attributed language as author bias, misapplied bias types, produced multiple false positives, missed several clear instances in the author’s own prose, and returned output inside code fences rather than a bare JSON array.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Two of four items explicitly come from quotes (“driving while brown,” “activist judges”), violating the rule to exclude quoted/attributed language.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Three of four flagged items are not genuine author-level bias: two are quotes; “None had criminal records.” is a factual statement and the omission claim is overreach.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed multiple clear author-prose spin/loaded choices such as “SOARING NUMBER OF IMMIGRANT DETAINEES,” “LANDSLIDE OF LAWSUITS,” “mass-deportation campaign,” “drive to lock up more immigrants,” and “jailing people indefinitely.”
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Mislabels include calling an attributed quote “Omission of Source Attribution,” and stretching “Bias by Omission” for a factual clause; only “sweeping legal rebuke” is reasonably labeled (though other labels like Spin/Subjective Adjectives might fit better).
